Summary:
- publisher=EIA, title=World Oil Transit Chokepoints, directness=high, why=authoritative baseline data on historical and projected oil transit through Strait of Hormuz, next=https://www.eia.gov/international/analysis/special-topics/World_Oil_Transit_Chokepoints  
- publisher=EIA, title=Country Analysis Brief: World Oil Transit Chokepoints (PDF), directness=high, why=latest updated analysis (June 2024) with granular transit volumes and disruption history; cites Vortexa and FACTS data, next=https://www.eia.gov/international/content/analysis/special_topics/World_Oil_Transit_Chokepoints/wotc.pdf  
- publisher=EIA, title=“The Strait of Hormuz is the world's most important oil transit chokepoint”, directness=high, why=confirms strategic significance and references tanker tracking data (Vortexa/FACTS) for 1H23, next=https://www.eia.gov/Todayinenergy/detail.php?id=61002  
- publisher=EIA, title=Short-Term Energy Outlook (STEO), directness=medium, why=flags recent market volatility linked to de facto closure scenarios — actionable for disruption modeling, next=https://www.eia.gov/outlooks/steo/pdf/steo_full.pdf  
- publisher=EIA, title=Homepage, directness=low, why=primary portal for accessing all EIA energy forecasts and petroleum data; verify latest reports via site search, next=https://www.eia.gov/
